findings.evidence: Lines 137-145: docstring says “Under the regular-cover quotient-group equivalence”; the statement calls `subgroupFiberOrbitQuotientEquivQuotientGroup` and has no `hp`/`hreg` hypotheses.
findings.fix: Change the docstring to say it is under the free-transitive deck-action quotient-group equivalence, or move the regular-cover wording only to the later `regularSubgroup...` lemma.
findings.issue: The docstring says this is under the regular-cover quotient-group equivalence, but the declaration uses `subgroupFiberOrbitQuotientEquivQuotientGroup` with only `[MulAction.IsPretransitive ...] [IsCancelSMul ...]`, not `regularSubgroupFiberOrbitQuotientEquivQuotientGroup` or `IsCoveringMap`/`IsRegular`.

findings.evidence: Lines 149-157: docstring says “Under the regular-cover quotient-group equivalence”; the statement uses `subgroupFiberOrbitQuotientEquivQuotientGroup` with only pretransitive/cancel typeclass assumptions.
findings.fix: Use wording matching `SubgroupFiberOrbitQuotientGroup.lean`, e.g. “Under the quotient equivalence, once the deck action on the fibre is free and transitive...”
findings.issue: The same inaccurate regular-cover wording appears on the `φ • e` version, although the theorem is again the general free-transitive equivalence, not the regular-cover wrapper.

summary: Two public lemma docstrings describe the general free-transitive quotient equivalence as the regular-cover specialization, which misstates the declarations' hypotheses and API role.
